name: context-pipeline-stress-test
description: Stress-tested v1 context pipeline with MiniMax-M2.5 at 16K and 64K windows. At 64K, pipeline stayed under 13% utilization (too roomy). At 16K (4K ops budget), pipeline correctly compacted/archived across 50 turns and 201K total input tokens (12.5x window recycled). Key findings: (1) budget ceiling enforcement works — utilization plateaus at exactly 25% during steady-state editing. (2) Failure turns blow past budget (67.4%) due to 1.0 significance score. (3) Compacted summaries lose TODO tracking — model completed 4/6 tools but claimed 6/6. Filed sapling-5e18 and sapling-227b.

content: Good stress test formula for context pipeline: multi-file repetitive task (edit N files one-at-a-time with test after each) at artificially small context window. 16K window with a 6-file task is the sweet spot — produces 50 turns, 9 compaction events, budget ceiling enforcement, and failure-retention edge cases. 64K is too roomy to trigger meaningful pipeline pressure. Use SAPLING_CONTEXT_WINDOW env var to set window size.
